description_long: This is a tall, narrow-necked terracotta vase with a bulbous lower body and a slightly flared rim. The surface is finished with a mottled, weathered texture in earthy tones, giving it a rustic, handmade appearance. Three raised horizontal bands encircle the upper body, adding visual interest and tactile detail. Around the widest part of the vase, there are evenly spaced oval protrusions that serve as decorative elements. The mouth of the vase is relatively small compared to its body, suggesting it is designed for holding a few stems or as a standalone decorative piece. The overall form and ornamentation evoke traditional pottery styles often seen in Mediterranean or Southwestern urban settings.
description: A tall, rustic terracotta vase with a narrow neck, raised horizontal bands, and oval decorative protrusions on its bulbous body.
description_view_0: Front view shows the vase's narrow neck, three raised bands, and a clear arrangement of oval protrusions on the widest part.
description_view_1: This view highlights the symmetry of the oval protrusions and the continuity of the horizontal bands around the vase.
description_view_2: The side view displays the same decorative features, with the mottled texture and banding clearly visible.
description_view_3: This angle emphasizes the vase's flared rim and the even spacing of the oval embellishments around the body.
